leads.name: Dariya Vyshnevska
leads.headline: Scaling B2B revenue with psychology-led sales systems. Helping teams close without pressure | Ex-Google, Ex-Grammarly
leads.comment: This feels like the in-between phase people rarely talk about. I’m in a similar reset right now. less about chasing numbers, more about building something I won’t want to run away from in a year.
leads.reason: First-person vulnerability: 'I'm in a similar reset right now... building something I won't want to run away from' — active forcing moment, ex-Google/Grammarly shows seniority and means.

leads.name: Hunter K. Jones, MA, MS
leads.headline: Founder & CEO | Business Development & Sales | Investigations & Operations | Federal Agent | US Air Force | Global Security | Insider Threat | PM | Strategist | Servant Leader | Veteran Advocate | Coach & Mentor
leads.comment: Kyle Poyar…great stuff. Look forward to networking with you in the future as I am year one into my consulting LLC. Crawl, walk, run for me as I still have a FT job. Keep doing your thing!!
leads.reason: Founder & CEO in year one of consulting LLC while still holding a full-time job — active forcing moment of career transition with real stakes.

leads.name: Chris Tottman
leads.headline: Partner at Notion Capital
leads.comment: Congratulations 👏I'm a massive fan as you know 💙 my first day today moving from "all free" to "free + paid" 😳🤞
leads.reason: Partner at Notion Capital describing their own first day transitioning from all-free to paid content — real vulnerability about a live transition with professional stakes.

leads.name: Yannik Hellmich
leads.headline: Product • Founder • ex-Meta
leads.comment:
Congrats Sven! Taking the leap is always terrifying, especially completely solo. The biggest drawback for me personally was how isolating it can be to work solo on your own company. That's something I'd never do again and look for a co-founder. 

And definitely second learning technical skills for people who have none or little. In basically any discipline it will make sure you can provide more value directly or in the very least better understand a part of your discipline that is otherwise is done by other people. 

For me this was picking up Game Development (C#), as well as SQL and the associated tools of that ecosystem. Huge bonus in any conversation where you exchange with other experts.
leads.reason: First-person vulnerability about how isolating solo founding is, saying they'd never do it again — ex-Meta founder describing real professional pain from going independent, active forcing moment.
